refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how latency is achieved, only that it must be compatible with safe operation. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to maintain the problem focus on safety without prescribing implementation details. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency level supports safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for latency in teleoperation feedback loops
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control algorithms
Shared resources causing delays in data processing
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or predictive algorithms, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints affecting latency
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing buffering delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive mechanisms to handle variable network conditions and potential latency spikes to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream for real-time telemetry data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface for data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as NGHE Solution is the main system architecture module designed to handle real-time processing; minimal diffusion expected as buffering and processing are centralized here.
Vehicle module may handle some data locally but is not primarily responsible for system-wide real-time processing; high diffusion due to data needing to be shared with other modules.
Teleoperation requires real-time data but focuses on control commands rather than overall data processing; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not on general data processing; high diffusion if containing DP here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing a system architecture prioritizing real-time data processing and minimal buffering, as it centralizes data handling and processing logic.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to data exchange requirements.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution module if too many responsibilities are centralized, possibly impacting modularity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks are significant, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate real-time data processing from buffering management, allowing clearer modular boundaries between processing (NGHE Solution) and data acquisition (Vehicle/Teleoperation).

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports the requirement for teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to data exchange requirements.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and centralized responsibility for the DP. However, some diffusion risk exists due to necessary interactions with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, which may handle related data but are not primarily responsible for the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) is feasible given the centralized architecture and defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined external interfaces supporting the required data flows for teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate real-time data processing from buffering management to reduce coupling and clarify modular boundaries between NGHE Solution (processing) and Vehicle/Teleoperation (data acquisition/control).
Monitor the potential overloading of the NGHE Solution module to maintain modularity and manage complexity as responsibilities are centralized.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current DP and IM adequately address the FR with feasible verification and clear allocation. However, the recommended revisions suggest refining the DP to separate real-time data processing from buffering management to reduce coupling and clarify modular boundaries. Given the high safety criticality and the noted diffusion risks, decomposing the FR into next-level sub-FRs focusing on these distinct aspects will help manage complexity, improve modularity, and mitigate risks.

revise.why: To explicitly separate real-time data processing from buffering management, reducing coupling and clarifying modular boundaries between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR at a high level.
Verification of latency requirements is feasible with the current architecture.
The NGHE Solution module has strong cohesion but some diffusion risk due to necessary interactions.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules due to data exchange requirements.
Risk of overloading the NGHE Solution module as responsibilities centralize.
evidence_needed: Detailed analysis of data flow separation between processing and buffering.
Verification results of latency measurements after decomposition.
Assessment of modularity and complexity post-refinement.
